text:
Once upon a time in a small, quiet town called Whispering Pines, there lived a curious old curragh named Curdie. Curdie wasn't a normal old curragh; he had this peculiar gift. Whenever he was alone in his cozy little cottage, he could hear the thoughts of the forest creatures around him. Despite this unusual talent, Curdie lived the quiet life—he had hobbies, tales he loved to spin to the local birds, and a garden growing his own dreams.

One serene morning, as he tended his roses, Curdie's thoughts drifted. It was a gentle slope known as The Happy Path. He fancied himself a detective of sorts—not solving mysteries, but piecing together the whispers of nature.

Suddenly, a robin perched atop a low oak flicked its head at him. "Ah," Curdie thought, "what's on your mind today?"
